sanskrit:
मत्कर्मकृन्मत्परमो मद्भक्तः सङ्गवर्जितः।
निर्वैरः सर्वभूतेषु यः स मामेति पाण्डव॥
transliteration: mat-karma-kṛn mat-paramo mad-bhaktaḥ saṅga-varjitaḥ / nirvairaḥ sarva-bhūteṣu yaḥ sa mām eti pāṇḍava //
literal_meaning: Whoever works only for Me, regards Me as the highest goal, is devoted to Me, is free from attachment, harbors no enmity toward any being—he comes to Me, O Pāṇḍava.
contextual_meaning: After the cosmic vision, Kṛṣṇa gives Arjuna a five-point existential safety-plan: dedicate action, hold nothing higher than the sacred, love without bargaining, drop clinging, and refuse hatred. Do these and the overwhelming vision integrates into daily life instead of traumatizing.
psychological_state: Post-awe stabilization: the psyche moves from numinous overwhelm to embodied, enemy-free engagement with the world.
trigger_scenario: You’ve just witnessed a life-changing event (intensive retreat, near-death, psychedelic journey) and need a simple, repeatable code to stay grounded and kind at work on Monday.
anchor_text: Work for the Divine alone, love without clinging, and harm no one.
